summary: Implemented all 4 phases of the discord bot reorg plan. Phase 1: git mv opencode-discord-bot/ (14 tracked files) to packages/opencode-discord-bot/, repointed src = ./opencode-discord-bot and header comments in packages/opencode-discord-bot.nix. Phase 2: created hosts/hamsa/default.nix mirroring hosts/nandi/default.nix and added extraModules = [ ./hosts/hamsa/default.nix ] to the hamsa mkHost call in flake.nix; both nandi and hamsa now evaluate services.discordBot.enable == true, nandi unchanged. Phase 3: updated docs/discord-bot.md, packages/README.md, modules/README.md, README.md tree diagram, and hosts/hamsa/README.md to the new source path and hamsa wiring; also fixed one stale comment-only path reference in modules/system/optional/discord-bot.nix surfaced by the completeness-check grep (no functional change). Phase 4: nix flake check passes across all nixosConfigurations; nixos-rebuild build --flake .#hamsa succeeds and the built discord-bot.service ExecStart resolves to the packaged opencode-discord-bot-0.1.0 console script, confirming the intended drift closure. The MANUAL sudo nixos-rebuild switch + systemctl/journalctl runtime-verification step is documented in the summary for the user (sudo is sandbox-denied in agent context).

artifacts.summary: Relocated 14-file bot source tree (from root opencode-discord-bot/)
New host wiring file enabling services.discordBot
Updated source path + hamsa wiring prose

decisions_made: Executed phases in plan order (1 then 2, both wave 1) so Phase 4's combined build exercised both changes in one pass

Kept modules/system/optional/discord-bot.nix's functional structure (sops, systemd, callPackage path) fully untouched per the plan's non-goal; only fixed two stale-path comment lines to satisfy the Phase 3 completeness-check verification criterion (zero grep hits) -- annotated as a deviation
Did not attempt sudo nixos-rebuild switch or systemctl/journalctl checks -- documented as the MANUAL user step in the summary per the plan's explicit non-goal
